response.justification: The candidate is a versatile senior engineer with strong testing culture, open-source contributions, and some high-scale systems exposure, but he does not meet the role's core minimum qualifications: his C++ experience is limited to a short stint at National Instruments and a thesis project (far from 8 years), his systems work is primarily in DLang and TypeScript rather than C++, and he has no demonstrated experience leading technical strategy for a cloud compute platform or formally mentoring cloud/DevOps engineers in a leadership capacity.
strengths: High-scale, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-practices background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus teaching workshops and code katas
Broad full-stack and infrastructure breadth (Kubernetes, Docker, AWS CDK, event-driven platforms) with long-running open-source ownership
concerns: Falls well short of the 8-year C++ requirement; primary systems languages are DLang and TypeScript
No formal technical-leadership or people-mentoring role on a cloud or compute platform team
Career trajectory is individual-contributor product engineering, not internal platform strategy or cross-team alignment

key_factors.factor: Lack of formal technical leadership experience
